title: leonia andina
intro: Leonia andina, also known as 'Cinchona', is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is a tree native to the Andes mountains in South America, traditionally used for its medicinal properties. The bark of the Leonia andina contains high concentrations of quinine, a compound used to treat malaria, as well as other alkaloids with anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant's herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, have been used to treat fever, rheumatism, and other health issues, while also providing antioxidant and antimicrobial benefits.
